title: ageratina mariarum
intro: Ageratina mariarum, also known as Mexican mint hill or Hierba Maria, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae), native to the mountainous regions of Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ageratina mariarum include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
